question_text: A family ordered two pizzas for dinner. Both pizzas were the same size. Each slice of pizza was the same size. This diagram shows the amount of pizza remaining after dinner. Which of these equations shows how to find the fraction of a whole pizza that was remaining after dinner?
visual_description: Two equal-sized circular pizzas, each divided into 8 slices. In the first pizza, 3 slices are shaded to show remaining pizza; in the second pizza, 2 slices are shaded to show remaining pizza.

question_text: Claire correctly worked out the answer to a subtraction problem. Which of these could be Claire’s problem and work?

answer_options.text: 3012 − 176 = 2836, with regrouping shown above the digits as 2 9 10
3012 − 176 = 3836, with regrouping shown above the digits as 9 10
3012 − 176 = 2826, with regrouping shown above the digits as 2 9 9

visual_description: Four separate written subtraction problems, each showing 3012 minus 176 with different regrouping marks above the digits and different answers: 3164, 2836, 3836, and 2826.

question_text: A student created this figure by drawing line segments and angles. Which line segments and angles did the student use to create the figure? Select the two correct answers.

answer_options.text: line segment PR
line segment PQ
line segment QS
angle SPQ
angle SQR
angle QSR

visual_description: Four-sided figure with vertices labeled P, Q, R, S in order. PQ is a vertical left side, QR a horizontal bottom side, RS a slanted right side, and SP a horizontal top side, forming a quadrilateral.

question_text: In which of these numbers does the 7 have a value that is ten times the value of the 7 in 9,176?
Which of these comparisons are correct? Select the two correct answers.
A triangle is shown. How many lines of symmetry does the triangle have? Enter your answer in the box.
visual_description: Isosceles right triangle with a right angle at the top vertex and base angles each labeled 45 degrees.

question_text: Which of these statements are correct? Select the three correct answers.

answer_options.text: 36 ÷ p = 4 has a related multiplication fact of p × 4 = 36.
36 ÷ p = 4 has a related multiplication fact of 36 × p = 4.
s ÷ 7 = 5 has a related multiplication fact of s × 5 = 7.
s ÷ 7 = 5 has a related multiplication fact of 7 × 5 = s.
72 ÷ 12 = a has a related multiplication fact of a × 12 = 72.
72 ÷ 12 = a has a related multiplication fact of a × 72 = 12.

question_text: There is a garden, a patio, and a flower bed in the backyard of a house. The garden, the patio, and the flower bed are each in the shape of a rectangle.

parts.label: A
parts.text: The garden has a length of 4 feet and a width of 8 feet, as shown in the diagram. What is the area, in square feet, of the garden?

parts.label: B
parts.text: The patio has a length of 5 feet and an area of 35 square feet. What is the width, in feet, of the patio? Show or explain how you got your answer.

parts.label: C
parts.text: The owner of the house thinks the garden and the patio have the same perimeter. Is the owner correct? Explain your reasoning.

parts.label: D
parts.text: The area of the flower bed is less than the area of the garden. The perimeter of the flower bed is equal to the perimeter of the patio. What could be the length and the width of the flower bed? Explain how you know your answer is correct.

visual_description: Rectangle labeled with width 8 ft along the bottom and height 4 ft along the right side.

question_text: A student drinks 2/5 liter of water each morning. Which of these is the total amount of water, in liters, the student drinks over 4 mornings?

answer_options.text: 6/5 liters
8/5 liters
8/10 liter
8/20 liter

question_text: A teacher has 8 sheets of stickers for decorating posters. Each sheet has 10 stickers. Each poster will have exactly 3 stickers. What is the greatest number of posters the teacher can decorate?

answer_options.text: 25 posters
26 posters
27 posters
28 posters

question_text: On field day, a student recorded his finish times for three different races. He also recorded the distances of four of his softball throws.

parts.label: A
parts.question_text:
The student’s finish times for the three races are shown.

• 3 minutes
• 4 minutes, 39 seconds
• 10 minutes, 17 seconds

Which table shows the student’s finish times, in seconds, for the three races?

answer_options.text:
Finish Times table with rows:
3 minutes — 180 seconds
4 minutes, 39 seconds — 179 seconds
10 minutes, 17 seconds — 617 seconds

Finish Times table with rows:
3 minutes — 180 seconds
4 minutes, 39 seconds — 279 seconds
10 minutes, 17 seconds — 617 seconds

Finish Times table with rows:
3 minutes — 180 seconds
4 minutes, 39 seconds — 279 seconds
10 minutes, 17 seconds — 6,017 seconds

Finish Times table with rows:
3 minutes — 300 seconds
4 minutes, 39 seconds — 439 seconds
10 minutes, 17 seconds — 1,017 seconds

parts.visual_description: Four labeled tables, each titled 'Finish Times' with two columns, 'Time' and 'Time in Seconds', and three rows listing the times for 3 minutes, 4 minutes 39 seconds, and 10 minutes 17 seconds with different values in the seconds column for options A, B, C, and D.

parts.label: B
parts.question_text: The distances of the student’s four softball throws were recorded using different units of measurement, as shown. Which of these shows the distances of the softball throws in the correct order from least to greatest?

answer_options.text: 8 feet, 100 inches, 3 yards, 120 inches
8 feet, 3 yards, 100 inches, 120 inches
100 inches, 120 inches, 8 feet, 3 yards
3 yards, 8 feet, 100 inches, 120 inches

parts.visual_description: Four labeled rectangles showing distances: "120 inches", "3 yards", "8 feet", and "100 inches".

visual_description: Four labeled tables, each titled 'Finish Times' with two columns, 'Time' and 'Time in Seconds', and three rows listing the times for 3 minutes, 4 minutes 39 seconds, and 10 minutes 17 seconds with different values in the seconds column for options A, B, C, and D.

question_text:
Lashawn knows this number sentence is true.

4 × 7 = 28

What is the value of the unknown number that makes this number sentence true?

4 × 700 = unknown number

Enter your answer in the box.
A protractor is used to measure angle A, as shown. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle A? Enter your answer in the box.
visual_description: Diagram of a protractor over a horizontal ray labeled A. A second ray extends upward from the vertex, forming an acute angle measured by the protractor.

question_text: What value of c makes this equation true? 4/6 = 8/c Enter your answer in the box.
A student uses squares and triangles to make a pattern. In each step of the pattern, the student adds 1 square and 2 triangles, as shown. The student continues the pattern.

parts.label: A
parts.text: What is the total number of triangles in Step 4 of the pattern?

parts.label: B
parts.text: What is the total number of squares in Step 6 of the pattern? Explain how you know your answer is correct.

parts.label: C
parts.text: What is the total number of triangles in Step 9 of the pattern? Explain how you can get your answer by using multiplication.

parts.label: D
parts.text: One step in the pattern will have a total of 64 triangles. What is the total number of squares in that step? Show or explain how you got your answer.

visual_description: Diagram labeled Step 1, Step 2, and Step 3 showing a growing pattern made from one square with a triangle above and below in Step 1, then two such square-and-two-triangle groups side by side for Step 2, and three groups side by side for Step 3.

question_text: Which of these sentences are correct? Select the three correct answers.

answer_options.text: The number 142,839 rounded to the nearest thousand is 142,000.
The number 142,839 rounded to the nearest thousand is 143,000.
The number 142,839 rounded to the nearest ten thousand is 140,000.
The number 142,839 rounded to the nearest ten thousand is 150,000.
The number 142,839 rounded to the nearest hundred thousand is 100,000.
The number 142,839 rounded to the nearest hundred thousand is 200,000.

question_text: Which two fractions are equivalent? Select the two correct answers.
Which of these shapes appear to have at least one pair of parallel sides and at least one pair of perpendicular sides? Select the three correct shapes.

answer_options.text: Quadrilateral with one pair of horizontal parallel sides; right side slanted inward.
Slanted parallelogram.
Square with horizontal top and bottom and vertical sides.
Rectangle drawn at a slant (tilted).
Trapezoid with a shorter horizontal top base and a longer horizontal bottom base; sides slant inward.

visual_description: Five labeled quadrilaterals A, B, C, D, and E: A is a trapezoid with one vertical and one slanted side; B is a slanted parallelogram; C is a square; D is a tilted rectangle; E is an isosceles trapezoid with top and bottom bases horizontal.

question_text: Which of these fractions is equivalent to 0.3?
Which fraction model is shaded to show the product of this expression? 4 × 1/3

answer_options.text: Two rectangles each divided into 3 equal horizontal parts; in the first rectangle all 3 parts are shaded, and in the second rectangle only the bottom part is shaded.
Two rectangles each divided into 6 equal horizontal parts; in the first rectangle the top 3 parts are shaded, and in the second rectangle the top 2 parts are shaded.
Two rectangles each divided into 6 equal horizontal parts; in the first rectangle the top part is shaded, and in the second rectangle no parts are shaded.
Two rectangles each divided into 4 equal horizontal parts; in the first rectangle the top 3 parts are shaded, and in the second rectangle the top 2 parts are shaded.

visual_description: At the top, a large rectangle labeled "represents 1 whole" contains an inner vertical bar that is fully shaded, indicating 1/3 of the whole. Below are four answer choices A–D, each showing a pair of rectangular fraction models with various horizontal partitions and shaded parts representing different fractions.
